content.problemStatement:
You are given two strings SSS and TTT, both of which contain only the letters 'AAA' and 'BBB'.

You can perform the following operation on string SSS:


Choose an index iii (1≤i≤N−3(1 \leq i \leq N-3(1≤i≤N−3) such that Si+1=Si+2S_{i+1} = S_{i+2}Si+1​=Si+2​ and at the same time Si≠Si+3S_i \neq S_{i+3}Si​=Si+3​.
Then, delete the characters at indices i+1i+1i+1 and i+2i+2i+2 from SSS. The two remaining parts are concatenated together.
For example, if S=AABBBAS = \texttt{AABBBA}S=AABBBA, and the operation is performed with i=2i = 2i=2 (which is valid since S2≠S5S_2 \neq S_5S2​=S5​ while S3=S4S_3 = S_4S3​=S4​), S3S_3S3​ and S4S_4S4​ will be deleted, turning the string into AABA\texttt{AABA}AABA.

You can perform the given operation as many times as you like (possibly, zero).

Is it possible to make SSS equal to TTT?


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer QQQ — the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN — the size of the string SSS, and MMM — the size of the string TTT.
The second line of each test case contains the string SSS, containing the letters 'AAA' and 'BBB' only.
The third line of each test case contains the string TTT, containing the letters 'AAA' and 'BBB' only.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: `YES` if it is possible to convert SSS into TTT, otherwise `NO`.


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e., the strings `NO`, `No`, `nO`, and `no` will all be treated as equivalent.
constraints: SSS and TTT contain only the characters `A` and `B`.
The sum of NNN and the sum of MMM over all test cases both won't exceed 3⋅1053\cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.input:
3
13 7
BBBBAAABAAAAA
BBABAAA
6 4
AAABBA
ABAB
10 4
ABBBAAAAAB
ABAB
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

We can apply operation at i=2i=2i=2, then the string becomes BBAAABAAAAA\texttt{BBAAABAAAAA}BBAAABAAAAA.
Then we can apply operation at i=3i=3i=3, then the string becomes BBABAAAAA\texttt{BBABAAAAA}BBABAAAAA.
Then we can apply operation at i=4i=4i=4, then the string becomes BBABAAA\texttt{BBABAAA}BBABAAA, which is equal to TTT.

Test case 222:

You can not convert SSS into TTT by using the operation any way or any number of times.
